statistics.name: assists
statistics.description: The number of times a player who passes the ball to a teammate in a way that leads to a score by field goal, meaning that he or she was "assisting" in the basket. There is some judgment involved in deciding whether a passer should be credited with an assist.

statistics.name: turnovers
statistics.description: The number of times a player loses possession to the other team.

statistics.description: The total number of minutes played.

statistics.name: assistTurnoverRatio
statistics.description: The average number of assists a player or team records per turnover

statistics.description: The total number of rebounds (offensive and defensive).

statistics.description: The number of times when the defense obtains the possession of the ball after a missed shot by the offense.

article.description: Kevin Mays scored a career-high 29 points and grabbed 13 rebounds to lift Cal State Bakersfield to an 81-68 win over Fresno Pacific on Friday.
article.headline: CS Bakersfield turns back Fresno Pacific 81-68
article.linkText: CS Bakersfield turns back Fresno Pacific 81-68
article.story: BAKERSFIELD, Calif. -- Kevin Mays scored a career-high 29 points and grabbed 13 rebounds to lift Cal State Bakersfield to an 81-68 win over Fresno Pacific on Friday. Damiyne Durham hit five 3-pointers and added 18 points for the Roadrunners (3-0). Mays hit 9 of 14 field-goal attempts and 11 of 15 free throws. Eight of his rebounds were on the offensive end as Bakersfield outrebounded the Sunbirds 39-29. Spencer Krannitz had 17 points and Tyrone Viney Jr., 15 with a 10-for-10 performance at the foul line in the game that was an exhibition for NAIA Fresno Pacific. Bakersfield led by a dozen at halftime and opened the second half with an 11-1 run to go up 54-32. Aly Ahmed scored the first four points with Durham contributing a 3 and Mays a tip-in. When the Sunbirds got within 11, Mays scored six straight points to make it 75-58 with 5:46 left.
